libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: For 2.0.0 tests, use the jakarta.activation package, not javax.activation. If testing mailcap or MIME-type file lookup, create deterministic temporary test resources and set/restore relevant system properties such as user.home inside the test instead of relying on host files.

deterministic_setup.reason:
The resolved jakarta.activation-api:2.0.0 POM states it is only for compiling against the API and excludes implementation-specific classes. Key API classes such as MailcapCommandMap and MimetypesFileTypeMap reference com.sun.activation.registries classes and default META-INF resources supplied by th
...[truncated]

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8769 [Automation] javac compile fails for jakarta.activation:jakarta.activation-api:2.0.0; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: The implementation jar contains duplicate jakarta.activation API classes as well as the missing com.sun.activation.registries classes; local Gradle and Native Image verification should confirm classpath ordering is acceptable.
The current compile failure excerpt is primarily caused by stale javax.activation imports from the 1.2.1 tests, so dependency setup alone will not fix the generated source.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: Jakarta Activation 2.0.0 is an API artifact; meaningful runtime coverage of common entry points needs the matching standalone implementation classes in addition to migrating tests from javax.activation to jakarta.activation.
